title: Hypertension and Headache - Preeclampsia with Severe Features
chief_complaint: 32-year-old G1P0 at 34 weeks with severe headache, blurred vision, and swelling in hands and face for 2 days. BP 160/100 mmHg on two readings 4 hours apart.

physical_exam.general: Facial edema, hand edema 2+, brisk reflexes 3+, no clonus

key_learning_points: Severe features: BP>160/110, HA, visual changes, RUQ pain
Steroids for fetal lung maturity if <37 weeks

patient_responses.headache: Worst headache of my life, painkillers don't help
patient_responses.vision: I see spots and flashing lights
patient_responses.swelling: My rings don't fit, face looks puffy

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Preeclampsia with severe features - this is serious.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is preeclampsia. BP + proteinuria + symptoms confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Preeclampsia: MgSO4 4-6g loading then 1-2g/hr. Labetalol or nifedipine for BP >160/110. Betamethasone for fetal lung maturity. Deliver after 34 weeks. Monitor for eclampsia and HELLP.
